doc.title: Raku Capture
doc.description: Capture a room with your phone and turn it into an explorable 3D Gaussian splat.
doc.helpDescription: Help for Raku Capture: how scanning works, camera troubleshooting, tips for a good scan, and how to connect a capture to an AI assistant.

header.protoBadgeTitle: Development prototype, not yet wired to a live backend

intro.heading: Capture a space
intro.lead: Point your phone at a room and slowly walk around it. Raku turns the footage into a Gaussian splat you can explore in 3D — on your phone, on Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses, and (later) on Android XR.
intro.previewCaption: Live sample — drag to look around
intro.previewCaptionNamed: Live sample: {name} — drag to look around
intro.cameraNote: Camera access is requested later, when you open the camera.

guide.heading: Walk the room
guide.lead: Cover the space from multiple angles. Tap each step as you go, or let auto-coverage track it.
guide.step1: Stand in a corner and pan slowly across the room
guide.step2: Walk the perimeter, keeping walls in frame
guide.step3: Capture furniture and objects up close
guide.step4: Look up and down to catch ceiling and floor

scale.heading: Add a size reference
scale.lead: A scan on its own has no real-world scale, so measurements are not possible from it. Pick something of a known size to place in view for a few seconds — the reconstruction can then work out true metric scale. No LiDAR needed.
scale.skipNote: On a phone with a depth sensor (LiDAR) you can skip this — scale comes from the sensor instead. Without a reference or a depth sensor, the scan has no scale and an AI assistant cannot measure it.

creditCard.name: Credit card
creditCard.desc: Any bank or ID card — 85.60 × 53.98 mm (ISO/IEC 7810). The most reliable everyday reference.

paperA4.name: A4 paper
paperA4.desc: A standard A4 sheet — 297 × 210 mm (ISO 216). Common outside the US.

paperLetter.name: US Letter paper
paperLetter.desc: A standard Letter sheet — 279.4 × 215.9 mm (8.5 × 11 in). Common in the US and Canada.

scale.frameLabel: Lay the {name} on a flat surface and hold it steady in the frame.
scale.frameDone: Reference captured — now scan the room.

capture.lowMovement: Step sideways and keep scanning — turning in place can't be reconstructed
capture.lowMovementConfirm: Low movement detected — reconstruction will likely fail. Tap OK to finish anyway, or Cancel to keep scanning.
capture.movementUnknown: Movement can't be measured on this device — be sure to step sideways while scanning, not just turn in place.
hints: Pan slowly across the room from one corner.
Walk the perimeter, keeping the walls in frame.
Move in close on furniture and objects.
Orbit the edges and corners — sweep a little past each border so the outline is fully covered (thin coverage there is what leaves stray spikes).
Tilt up and down to catch the ceiling and floor.
Great coverage — tap Finish capture when ready.

processing.labelDefault: This usually takes a minute or two…
processing.slowHint: Still working — slow scenes can take 20+ minutes. We will not give up while the server is making progress.
processing.computeNotice: Reconstruction runs on our shared compute and can take several minutes right now — we are scaling up GPU capacity. Your scan is still processing.
processing.computeStillWorking: This is taking longer than usual, but your scan is still being processed. We are compute-limited right now, not broken — thanks for your patience.
processing.gpuWorkerDown: Heads-up: no cloud GPU worker is online right now — this capture will likely fail until one comes back.

viewer.metaControls: Drag to orbit · scroll or pinch to zoom
viewer.removeFloatersTitle: Hide stray floaters and needle spikes in the live view (tap again to undo)
viewer.declutterDone: Removed {n} stray splats — tap again to undo.
viewer.declutterNone: No obvious floaters found — this scan already looks clean.
viewer.declutterUnavailable: Auto-clean needs the live 3D view — use Clean up to edit in SuperSplat.
viewer.cleanupTitle: Open this scan in the SuperSplat editor to trim floaters and crop
viewer.shareCopyFailed: Could not copy the URL — copy it from the address bar

cleanup.openingPly: Opening SuperSplat with your splat loaded — trim floaters, then re-export.
cleanup.openedCopied: SuperSplat opened. Splat URL copied — paste or drag it into the editor to clean up.
cleanup.openedManual:
SuperSplat opened. Open this scan there to clean up:
{url}

error.title: Try again
error.cameraDenied: Camera permission was denied. Enable it in your browser settings to capture a room.
error.cameraUnsupported: Camera capture is not supported in this browser, or the page is not served over HTTPS.
error.noFrames: No frames were captured — try scanning the room again.
error.uploadUnreadable: Upload succeeded but the response was unreadable.
error.reconstructionLostContact: Lost contact with the reconstruction job.
error.diagSfm: We couldn't line up enough of your photos. Re-scan slowly, keeping lots of overlap and covering all the walls.
error.diagTimeout: Your scene was large and timed out on our current capacity — try a smaller area or fewer surfaces.
error.diagNetwork: The upload was interrupted. Check your connection and tap Try again.
error.diagUnknown: We hit an unexpected error. You can try again, or open the technical details below to see what the server reported.
error.phoneGpuUnsupported: Note: phone GPU mode is not supported by this browser.

help.pageLead: Everything you need to scan a space well, fix a capture that went wrong, and connect the result to an AI assistant.
help.headsUpBody: Raku Capture is an early prototype. The capture flow, camera, upload and viewer are real, but the reconstruction backend is currently simulated — a finished scan returns a placeholder 3D splat, not a photoreal reconstruction of your room. The native photogrammetry + Gaussian-splat training backend is still in development. Treat the result as a working preview of the flow, not a finished 3D scan.
help.howIntro: A scan moves through a few stages. You can watch each one happen on screen.
help.howStep1Body: A short checklist guides you: pan from a corner, walk the perimeter, get close to furniture, look up and down.
help.howStep2Body: The live camera grabs still keyframes as you move (capped at 48). A coverage bar fills as the app sees more of the space; "Finish capture" unlocks once you have enough.
help.howStep3Body: The keyframes are sent to the Raku reconstruction service. The progress bar tracks the real upload.
help.howStep4Body: The service works through your frames and builds the 3D result. This usually takes a minute or two.
help.howStep5Body: The result opens in an interactive 3D viewer — drag to orbit, scroll or pinch to zoom. From here you can share it or connect it to an AI assistant.
help.howPrivacy: Nothing is captured before you open the camera, and frames only leave your device at the upload step.
help.cameraIntro: Raku Capture needs camera access to scan. The browser asks for permission the first time you tap Open camera on the walk-the-room step — not before. If the camera will not start, work through these:
help.cameraNoPromptItem1: Use a secure connection. Cameras only work over https:// (or localhost). If the address bar shows http://, the browser will silently block the camera.
help.cameraNoPromptItem2: You may have dismissed it. Reload the page and tap Open camera again.
help.cameraBlockedIntro: Once blocked, the browser remembers it and will not ask again — you have to clear it manually:
help.cameraBlockedItem1: iOS Safari: Settings → Safari → Camera, or tap the aA / page-settings icon in the address bar and set Camera to Allow, then reload.
help.cameraBlockedItem2: Android Chrome: tap the lock / tune icon left of the address bar → Permissions → Camera → Allow, then reload.
help.cameraBlockedItem3: Desktop Chrome / Edge: click the lock icon in the address bar → Site settings → Camera → Allow.
help.cameraInUseTitle: "Camera in use" or a black preview
help.cameraInUseItem1: Close other apps or browser tabs that might hold the camera (video calls, other camera tabs).
help.cameraInUseItem2: On a phone, fully close and reopen the browser.
help.cameraInUseItem3: If you installed Raku Capture as an app (PWA), the OS may track its camera permission separately — check the app's entry in your system settings.
help.cameraFrontBody: Raku Capture asks for the rear camera, which is the right one for scanning a room. If your device starts on the front camera anyway, it usually has only one usable camera, or the browser ignored the request — the scan still works, just point the device deliberately.
help.tipsIntro: The reconstruction is only as good as the footage. A few habits make a large difference.
help.tipsDoItem1: Light the room evenly. Bright, diffuse light works best. Turn on the lights; open the curtains.
help.tipsDoItem2: Move slowly and steadily. A slow, smooth pan gives the app sharp frames. Think "guided tour", not "quick glance".
help.tipsDoItem3: Cover the space from many angles. Walk the perimeter, and capture each object from two or three sides.
help.tipsDoItem4: Keep overlap. Each frame should share plenty with the last — do not jump across the room.
help.tipsDoItem5: Get close to detail. Furniture, corners, and textured surfaces give the reconstruction features to lock onto.
help.tipsDoItem6: Look up and down. Catch the ceiling and the floor, not just eye-level walls.
help.tipsAvoidItem1: Glass, mirrors, and shiny surfaces. They confuse reconstruction badly — reflections look like fake geometry. Skip them where you can, or capture them from a steep angle.
help.tipsAvoidItem2: Fast motion and quick turns. Motion blur loses frames.
help.tipsAvoidItem3: Blank walls. A featureless white wall gives the algorithm nothing to track. Include a corner or an object in frame.
help.tipsAvoidItem4: Dim or flickering light. Low light means noisy, blurry frames.
help.tipsAvoidItem5: Moving things mid-scan. People, pets, and curtains that move between frames create artifacts. Scan a still room.
help.tipsScaleBody: A plain scan has no built-in sense of scale, so an AI assistant cannot give you absolute sizes from it. To get metric measurements, include something of a known size in the scan (a door, a sheet of paper, a tape measure), or use a device that records depth. If scale is missing, the assistant will say so and ask you to add a reference rather than guess.
help.failedIntro: Sometimes a scan ends on the error screen instead of the 3D viewer. That is recoverable — here is what to check.
help.failedRetryBody: Tap Try again on the error screen. Uploads and the reconstruction service can fail transiently — a clean retry often works. If it fails twice in a row, the cause is more likely your footage or your connection.
help.failedKeepsItem1: Re-scan with more coverage. Too few frames, or frames that all look at the same wall, leave the reconstruction nothing to solve. Walk the whole perimeter and fill the coverage bar before tapping "Finish capture".
help.failedKeepsItem2: Re-scan more slowly. Blurry frames from fast motion are the most common cause of a failed solve.
help.failedKeepsItem3: Remove the hard surfaces. A room dominated by glass, mirrors, or blank walls may simply not reconstruct. Add texture and detail to the scan.
help.failedKeepsItem4: Check your connection. A dropped upload shows as a failure. Get onto stable Wi-Fi and retry.
help.failedKeepsItem5: Start a fresh capture. If retry stays stuck, tap to begin a new scan rather than reusing the failed one.
help.failedHonestBody: Because the reconstruction backend is currently simulated, today's "result" is a placeholder rather than a true scan of your room — and a failure is usually an upload, connection, or service hiccup rather than a verdict on your footage. The scanning habits above still matter: they are what will make a real difference once the photogrammetry backend is live.
help.connectHeading: Connect the result to an AI assistant
help.connectIntro1: Once a scan reaches the 3D viewer, you can hand it to an AI assistant such as Claude, or any assistant that speaks the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and ask questions about the space: how big the room is, whether a sofa fits along a wall, how far apart two windows are.
help.connectIntro2: The assistant does not eyeball the answer. It reads the scene through MCP tools and runs deterministic geometry, then quotes the computed numbers, so the same question always gives the same answer.
help.connectShortItem1: Install the Raku MCP server (a small Python package from the raku-runtime repository).
help.connectShortItem2: Paste one config block into your assistant's MCP settings and restart it.
help.connectShortItem3: Ask the assistant to load your captured space and answer spatial questions about it.
help.connectShortFootnote: Full step-by-step instructions, the exact config to paste, and a description of every tool live in Connect RakuAI to Claude in 2 minutes (docs/capture/CONNECT-IN-2-MINUTES.md in the raku-runtime repository).
help.connectAsk1: "How big is this room?" — floor area.
help.connectAsk3: "Will a king bed fit along the longest wall?"
help.connectAsk4: "Does a desk fit under the window?"
help.connectAsk5: "How far apart are the two windows?"
help.connectAsk6: "Show me the room without the partition."
help.connectCaveat1: The scene is currently simulated. Until the photogrammetry backend lands, the assistant reasons over a synthetic placeholder scene, not a true reconstruction of your room. The connection, the tools, and the geometry are all real — the room data they read is not yet.
help.connectCaveat2: No scale, no measurements. If your scan has no known-size reference, the assistant will not invent numbers. It will tell you the scale is unknown and ask you to add a reference and re-scan. That is intentional honesty, not a bug.
help.faqA1: Nothing leaves your device until the upload step. At that point the captured keyframes are sent to the Raku reconstruction service to be turned into a 3D result.
help.faqQ2: Why does the result look like a placeholder?
help.faqA2: Because it is one. The reconstruction backend is still in development; today a finished scan returns a stand-in 3D splat so the full flow is testable. Photoreal reconstruction is coming — this prototype is honest about not being there yet.
help.faqA3: Usually a minute or two. If the processing screen sits far longer than that, cancel and start a fresh capture.
help.faqQ4: Can I scan outdoors or a very large space?
help.faqA4: Capture works best on a single, well-lit, enclosed room. Large or outdoor spaces are harder to cover with overlapping frames and are more likely to reconstruct poorly.
help.faqQ5: Does this work on AR glasses?
help.faqA5: Raku Capture is built as an installable web app, which is the entry path for Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses and (later) Android XR. The phone PWA is the supported target today.
help.faqQ6: Do I need a developer account to connect an AI assistant?
help.faqA6: No account, but today you do need to run the Raku MCP server locally — see docs/capture/CONNECT-IN-2-MINUTES.md. A zero-install hosted connector is planned for a later phase.
help.footer: Raku Capture is an early prototype — the reconstruction backend is simulated.

auth.signedOut: Sign in to capture — access requires an approved account.
auth.accessRequested: Access requested — an admin will review it and email you a temporary password to sign in with.
auth.pendingApproval: Your access request is pending approval — you'll be able to sign in once an admin approves it.

signin.heading: Sign in to Raku
signin.lead: Capture access requires an approved account. Sign in with the password we emailed you.
signin.toOtp: Sign in with an email code instead

otp.heading: Sign in with an email code
otp.lead: Enter your email and we'll send you a 6-digit sign-in code. No password or passkey needed — works on any device.
otp.sent: If that account exists, we emailed a 6-digit code. Enter it below.
otp.needEmail: Enter your email to get a code.
otp.needCode: Enter the 6-digit code we emailed you.
otp.failed: Could not sign you in — please try again.
otp.network: Could not reach the server — check your connection and try again.

register.heading: Request access
register.lead: Request access with your email. An admin reviews each request — once approved, we email you a temporary password to sign in with.
register.terms: I agree to the terms and privacy policy.
register.toSignin: Already have an account? Sign in

changepw.heading: Set a new password
changepw.lead: You signed in with a temporary password. Choose a new password to finish setting up your account.
changepw.mismatch: The new passwords do not match.
changepw.failed: Could not change your password — check the temporary password and try again.
changepw.network: Could not reach the server — check your connection and try again.

compute.title: Where do you want to process this?

cloud.title: Cloud (recommended)
cloud.desc: We reconstruct on our servers. Works on any device. Takes ~2-20 minutes depending on the scene.

phone.title: Phone GPU (experimental)
phone.desc: For testing: reconstruct on this device. Unless you have a high-end smartphone (iPhone 15 Pro+, Pixel 8 Pro+, Galaxy S24 Ultra+), select Cloud - phone GPU is currently in beta and slow on older devices.

compute.fallback: Your browser doesn't support WebGPU yet. Falling back to Cloud automatically.

demo.heading: Explore a sample capture
demo.lead: Pick a pre-captured sample scene and explore it in 3D — drag to orbit, scroll or pinch to zoom. These are pre-baked samples, not live scans: live phone-scan reconstruction is paused right now.
demo.offlineNote: Each sample is cached on this device after its first view, so the demo keeps working offline.
demo.sampleSize: {size} download — cached for offline replay after the first view
demo.unknownSample: There is no demo sample called "{id}" — pick one below.
demo.manifestError: Could not load the sample list. If this device is offline, open this page once while online to enable the offline demo.
demo.splatUnavailableOffline: "{name}" is not available offline yet — this device has not cached it. Open it once while online and it will replay offline.
demo.splatUnreachable: Could not load "{name}" — the sample host is unreachable. Check the connection and try again.
demo.noLiveCapture: Demo mode: live capture and reconstruction are disabled — nothing was uploaded.

debug.title: Capture debug log
debug.sendFailed: couldn't reach server — try again or use Copy
debug.shipFailed: diagnostics not reaching server — use Copy to share logs
